critic_raw:
Assessor A: AUROC vs. threshold-specific metrics: the former is appropriate for development; the latter is required for deployment. The paper conflates these. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor B: The nursing capacity constraint (2 per shift per 30-bed unit) is operationally critical and completely unaddressed. Alert burden characterization is a pre-deployment requirement. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Assessor C: AUROC 0.88 is promising. PPV, alert rate, and lead time at the operational threshold are the next required data points before deployment. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

Synthesis: Unanimous. ETD specified.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed, consistent across all three ensemble runs.

empirical_test.measure: PPV, alert rate per patient-day, and median lead time at the 2-alerts-per-30-bed-unit-per-shift threshold
empirical_test.success_criterion: PPV is acceptable and alert rate within capacity on retrospective data
empirical_test.failure_criterion: PPV at constrained threshold is below clinical utility or alert rate exceeds nursing capacity
